prompt:
Metrics pipeline ticket. Our latency aggregator currently keeps sum and sum-of-squares per shard and computes variance at flush time. On the new gateway (timestamps-as-floats territory, values with a giant common offset) it's been reporting NEGATIVE variances, which is how I learned about catastrophic cancellation the hard way. Please build the replacement properly: Welford's online algorithm, single pass, no storing the stream.

`streamstats.py`, class `RunningStats`:
- `add(x)` folds in one observation and returns self (we chain these in the collectors).
- `count`, `mean`, `variance`, `std`, `min`, `max` — variance is SAMPLE variance (n-1 denominator; this feeds confidence intervals downstream, don't give me the population one). `mean` on an empty accumulator is a ValueError; `variance`/`std` need at least two points, else ValueError. `min`/`max` are None when empty.
- `merge(other)` — this is the important one. Shards accumulate independently and the flusher combines them. It must return a NEW accumulator (mutating either input has caused real double-count bugs here), handle empty operands on either side, and produce count/mean/variance/min/max indistinguishable from having streamed everything into one accumulator. Use the parallel-variance combination formula, not "replay one into the other".

The tests feed a large-offset dataset where the old sum-of-squares approach loses everything, and cross-check a 10k-value stream against an exact two-pass computation — so the numerics are the acceptance criteria, not an implementation nicety. `python3 test_streamstats.py`, don't edit it, stdlib only (math is fine, statistics is cheating).
